name: Cranberry Short Ribs
recipeIngredient: 4.5 lb bone-in beef short ribs English-cut, 6–8 pieces
2.5 tsp kosher salt divided
1 tsp black pepper freshly ground
2 tbsp neutral oil canola or avocado
1 large yellow onion diced
2 carrots peeled and diced
2 celery stalks diced
4 cloves garlic smashed
2 tbsp tomato paste for richness
1 cup dry red wine optional; sub beef broth
1.5 cups beef broth low-sodium
1 cup 100% cranberry juice unsweetened or blend
1 cup fresh or frozen cranberries no need to thaw
0.5 cup dried cranberries adds body and sweetness
2 tbsp soy sauce or tamari for GF
2 tbsp maple syrup or packed brown sugar
0.5 tsp ground allspice or 1 star anise
1 strip orange zest 3-inch strip or 1 tsp grated
1 bay leaf
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar to finish
1 tbsp unsalted butter optional, for gloss
2 tbsp fresh parsley chopped, for garnish
recipeInstructions: If time allows, season ribs with 2 tsp kosher salt and pepper and refrigerate uncovered 8–24 hours for deeper flavor. If not, proceed directly to searing.
Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high, add oil, and sear ribs in 2–3 batches, 3–4 minutes per side, until deeply browned. Transfer to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion, carrots, and celery; cook 5–6 minutes, scraping browned bits. Stir in garlic and tomato paste; cook 2 minutes to caramelize paste.
Deglaze with red wine (or 1 cup broth), simmer 2–3 minutes to reduce by half.
Add remaining broth, cranberry juice, fresh and dried cranberries, soy/tamari, maple syrup, allspice, orange zest, and bay leaf. Bring to a simmer.
Return ribs and accumulated juices to the pot, meat side down. Liquid should come halfway up the ribs; add a splash of broth if needed. Cover.
Transfer to oven and braise 2.5–3 hours, turning ribs once halfway, until fork slides in with little resistance (195–205°F internal for tenderness).
Carefully move ribs to a tray. Strain braising liquid through a fine-mesh strainer, pressing on solids. Discard bay and zest. Skim fat or use a fat separator.
Return strained liquid to pot and simmer over medium-high 10–12 minutes until reduced to a glossy sauce that coats a spoon (about 2 cups). Stir in balsamic and butter.
Taste and season with remaining 0.5 tsp kosher salt as needed. Return ribs to the pot and spoon glaze over; simmer 3–5 minutes to rewarm and lacquer.
Garnish with parsley and serve with mashed potatoes, polenta, or roasted roots.
author: heygool.com
notes: Cranberries vary in tartness; balance sauce at the end with extra maple or a splash of vinegar.
